unit.note: F0 gate green both workspaces. Server vitest 165 files / 1918 passed + 1 skipped (1919) (SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local) + tsc --noEmit clean + build green. Client vitest 203 files / 1972 passed + build green + tsc clean. New F15 coverage: server run-config-reader.transient.test.ts (3: stat EPERM with fresh last-good -> cached ok; last-good survives a full 5s poll gap; non-retryable EIO with no cache still invalid) + client useRunConfig.test.ts (6: invalid keeps polling at 10s; in_progress 5s; needs_validation 60s; complete/failed off; missing/v1_legacy off; undefined off). The run-config-reader.test.ts TTL-expiry boundary was moved 6s -> 31s (TTL raised 5s -> 30s).

integration.note: Confidence-calibration probes of the touches_io_boundary run-config read surface (producer=orchestrator atomic run_config rewrite -> file -> consumer readRunConfig). (1) Injected-deps unit round-trips (committed): stat EPERM + fresh last-good -> served ok; last-good survives a 5s poll gap; EIO-no-cache -> invalid; existing read/parse retry + TTL-expiry suite green. (2) THROWAWAY real-fs probe (run then deleted): producer writes real config -> readRunConfig with DEFAULT deps (real fs.stat + fs.readFile) -> ok (runId run-a1b2c3d4); malformed-but-readable file WITH a fresh cache -> invalid (NOT stale, proving stale-serve is bounded to physical faults); removed file -> missing (ENOENT->null); rewrite valid -> ok (cache refreshes to new content). PROBE PASS. Two consecutive clean probe rounds (probe set 2 found no issues after the fix) => asymptote reached; the real-fs probe falsified the plan-review TTL-staleness worry.

e2e.note: No Playwright run. The only client change is a pure poll-cadence function (runConfigPollIntervalMs in client/src/hooks/useRunConfig.ts feeding TanStack Query's refetchInterval) - no DOM/prop/render, no route/component, no Vite-config/proxy/WS-origin/CORS surface. Covered by 6 deterministic client unit tests + the full 1972-test jsdom suite (mounts real components) + client build. A browser smoke exercises nothing new. F0.5 surface=none (see surface_verification).

surface_verification.justification: HONEST client-touch disclosure: this iterate DOES touch a client `.ts` file (client/src/hooks/useRunConfig.ts) - detect_frontend_changes.py classifies any .ts under client/src/ as frontend by path, so it would report has_frontend_changes=true once committed (NOT claimed false). However the modification is a headless pure function: runConfigPollIntervalMs returns the number/false that TanStack Query uses for refetchInterval. There is no DOM/render/prop/visual change, no new route/component, and no Vite-config/proxy/WS-origin/CORS surface. It is fully covered by 6 new deterministic client unit tests, the full 1972-test client jsdom suite (which mounts real components importing the hook), a clean tsc --noEmit, and a green client build. AC2 correctness is proven RED-first on pre-fix main (invalid -> false latched polling OFF; now -> 10s). A browser_verify run would add no signal the client build + jsdom suite lack, so F0.5 surface=none - no fabricated browser run.

behaviors.behavior: AC1 - a transient run-config read no longer vanishes the pipeline lane: the existence stat probe now runs through the same retryTornRead envelope as the read path and falls back to last-good on exhaustion (was: bare try/catch -> instant invalid on any non-ENOENT fault); LAST_GOOD_TTL_MS 5000 -> 30000 (comfortably above the 5s poll cadence so a torn poll never expires the cache at fallback); client runConfigPollIntervalMs keeps polling on invalid at a 10s backoff instead of latching OFF.
behaviors.evidence: server run-config-reader.transient.test.ts (stat EPERM + fresh last-good -> served ok with a last-good warning; last-good survives a full 5s poll gap; EIO-no-cache still invalid) + run-config-reader.test.ts (existing read/parse retry + last-good + TTL-expiry@31s green) + client useRunConfig.test.ts (invalid -> 10s; in_progress 5s; needs_validation 60s; terminal/missing/v1_legacy/undefined off). Real-fs round-trip probe confirms malformed-readable -> invalid (not stale) and rewrite -> fresh.

behaviors.behavior: AC2 - a NEW regression proof is RED on pre-fix main and GREEN after
behaviors.evidence: RED captured on pre-fix main: server transient.test.ts 'stat throws EPERM' -> expected 'invalid' to be 'ok'; 'last-good survives a 5s poll gap' -> expected 'invalid' to be 'ok' (2 failed); client useRunConfig.test.ts 'keeps polling on a transient invalid' -> expected false to be truthy (1 failed). GREEN after the fix (server transient 3 passed; client useRunConfig 6 passed).

behaviors.behavior: AC3 - full server + client suites + builds stay green (no invariant/ADR regressed)
behaviors.evidence: Server vitest 165 files / 1918 passed + 1 skipped (SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local) + tsc + build green; client vitest 203 files / 1972 passed + tsc + build green. no-cross-package-imports.test.ts green (helper added to the SERVER types mirror only; client lib mirror untouched - no client-bundle pollution). Read-only-observer rules 1/12 intact (no writes to shipwright_run_config.json or ~/.claude/projects/**).

behaviors.behavior: AC4 - files stay within the expected footprint; bloat baseline not ratcheted
behaviors.evidence: run-config-reader.ts 438 -> 431 (< 439 baseline current; the retry envelope shrank it, no ratchet); types/run-config-v2.ts 245 -> 297 (< 300, not baselined - the sanctioned parseRunMode home for reader-ceiling helpers); new cohesive run-config-reader.transient.test.ts 102 (< 300) instead of pushing run-config-reader.test.ts past 300 (campaign guardrail); useRunConfig.ts 45 -> 57; new useRunConfig.test.ts 54. Footprint deviations vs the spec's 4-file list: (a) types module - EXPLICITLY directed by the brief + spec Fix direction; (b) new transient test file - explicitly permitted when a footprint test hits the ceiling; (c) useContinuePipeline.test.ts - FORCED by AC3 (its pre-existing poll block asserted invalid->false, moved to useRunConfig.test.ts). Serial #10, deps/blocks none, D01-D09 merged -> no live parallel collision. shipwright_bloat_baseline.json unchanged.
